summary: Found critical correctness issues that can lead to invalid ECS env values and unsafe multi-instance SQLite access; fixes provided.

issues.description: ECS environment variables must be strings; non-string values in local.all_env will serialize as numbers/booleans and cause task definition registration errors.
issues.current_code:
```hcl
environment = [for k, v in local.all_env : { name = k, value = v }]
```
issues.fixed_code:
```hcl
environment = [for k, v in local.all_env : { name = k, value = tostring(v) }]
```
issues.explanation: ECS expects all environment values to be strings; tostring() ensures numbers/booleans are converted, preventing task definition failures.

issues.description: The service allows desired_count > 1, which can start multiple tasks and corrupt the SQLite DB on shared EFS, violating the single-instance safety requirement.
issues.explanation: Clamping desired_count prevents accidental multi-instance deployments, and the precondition fails fast if an unsafe value is provided.
